essence: Fragmented meetup side-chat on model economics — attendees describe using cheaper models for basic data processing, the free Gemini tier to kick off tasks like slide prep, and open-source code with an agents SDK for personal home systems.

At Nick's Agents in the Wild session 4 (orchestration frameworks) David live-demos his OMI pipeline — phone-side transcription synced over Tailscale/SSH to the M4 Mini and rendered JSON-to-HTML — plus the Joy Juice bilingual menu research and AI QR-code experiments for Joy's nail-and-drink shop.

David approves the AWB registry doc with two edits — name it just 'registry' (drop 'workflow'), and the Angela feedback doc must tell her the auto-seeded config file exists and that she must update it with the path to her YAML document.

actions.text: Update Angela feedback document to flag that the registry config file exists and needs her YAML path after first-install auto-seeding

essence: Marketing idea for Joy's juice shop: a 2.5m tent pop-up juice stall at the busy Friday night market with flyers directing customers to the main shop, benchmarked against a nearby high-traffic shop reportedly turning over ~250k baht a day.

Long working session at Joy's shop defining the Joy Juice menu architecture — 6-8 base fruits, four drink categories (traditional Thai smoothies, Western/farang smoothies, signature blends, functional shots), a bilingual JSON+HTML menu with hover English/Thai toggle, AI background research on Western chains like Boost, and a daily testing protocol of two drinks poured into ~28 45ml shots given away with feedback recorded via OMI; Joy needs precise Western recipes from David since she owns the Thai side.

actions.text: Build and maintain the bilingual English/Thai drinks JSON document plus the hover-toggle HTML menu page with category tabs
Run the daily ~4pm testing protocol: two drinks, 28 sample shots given away outside 7-Eleven, feedback recorded via OMI for AI analysis
Visit competitor juice bars (Juiced in Nimman, Khun Kai Juice) for research

essence: Debugging the AWB–Signal Studio integration reveals the registry JSON work was lost/reverted, Moments That Matter is hard-coded and missing from the config screen, API examples are wrong across all three workflows (should be inferred from the YAML source of truth), and styling is inconsistent — David orders re-implementation of the per-user registry (Angela: Windows, no YouTube Launch Optimizer; David: Mac, with it), commit+push, and a Playwright MCP style review of every page on the live server at port 6040.

actions.text: Re-implement the configurable git-ignored registry JSON with prepopulated Angela (Windows, no YLO) and David (Mac, with YLO) example files
Run Playwright MCP against the live server on port 6040 to review all pages and propose look-and-feel fixes
Make restart/error messages adaptive to source workflow and application (Signal Studio vs FliHub)

essence: Port cleanup: SupportSignal runs 6000/6001 and Studio Signal 6040/6041, the new AWB (wrongly grabbing 3001) must get its own registered ports distinct from the still-active old AWB per the appydave brains port conventions; plus field-to-field mapping between two systems is rejected in favour of domain-specific translation at the call boundary, and Alex's prompt-review feedback is handed off for background prompt updates.

actions.text: Register correct SupportSignal client ports and choose+register distinct ports for the new AWB instance (old vs new AWB both active)
Apply Alex's prompt-review feedback in a background task and report back changes

essence: With three weeks left David de-scopes Angela's eight-step shift-management design to a future one-month feature to shop to clients, and locks Moments That Matter to a simple signal-only model — static participant-onboarding context plus one-or-batched free-text moments run through parallel predicate/classifier/observation analysis rows dynamically gated by participant flags — assigning Penny the prompts/schemas, Alex the YAML (one workflow with a batch-mode flag), background agents the AWB intake plus Signal Studio send-to-AWB, and a git-ignored per-machine registry.json with Angela/David examples; also flags Ralphie-plugin vs Anthropic /loop naming confusion and demands meaningful loop logging.

actions.text: Draft Moments That Matter prompts and schemas (Penny) and the YAML workflow with batch-mode flag (Alex) following the new-incident analysis-row pattern
Build AWB intake so Moments That Matter appears as a third selectable workflow and Signal Studio can push moments and incidents into it
Design per-machine registry.json of workflow base folders, git-ignored with Angela and David example files
Fix Ralphie loop logging and document the Ralphie plugin vs Anthropic /loop distinction

essence: David requests a researched plan for a unified app-startup capability — one skill/command that launches his everyday apps (FliHub, FliDeck, DeckHand, ThumbRack, more coming) with port-conflict handling, single-instance enforcement outside development, and auto browser open, working from the flivideo, apps, and brains directory trees.

actions.text: Research and propose the unified startup capability (ports, single instance, auto browser open) across the three directory structures

essence: Process question to the agent mid-workflow-development: when a blocker like non-persisting style data appears, is it captured as a backlog item, does it block the workflow, and should we stop-fix-redo or continue?

The loop that should be running at three-minute intervals is producing no useful feedback, so it gets restarted, and David asks for the two applications to be started directly rather than via Playwright MCP.
Request for two prompts each on the architecture and apps topics, reframed for a short five-page presentation deck format.
Designing NotebookLM visual-generation prompts for the 'airport LLM' architecture metaphor — three prompts each from the general, two-upstream-apps, and architecture perspectives.
